title: Where were the Angels
wikitext:
[]: Where were the Angels?             Quote Post by DEMOLITION D+ » Sat Aug 08, 2009 8:03 am Before they all decided to show up, is it ever revealed via classified files or something like that where the Angels have been hiding this whole time? I know a majority of them come out of the ocean, so I think the bottom of the ocean would be a good guess. But then again there were the angels that appeared in orbit, and there's Tabris.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Aug 08, 2009 8:07 am "dunno lol" is all we know from thematic pov it's quite important where they come from is shrouded in mystery but in mythoswise it can be quite painful...

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Aug 08, 2009 8:56 am The two that come from space (Sahaqiel and Arael) may have been blown out into orbit by Second Impact, most of the others probably landed in the ocean, and Sandalphon presumably landed in the volcano.

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Sat Aug 08, 2009 9:46 am Why the thought that they were all there during second impact?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Aug 08, 2009 2:42 pm Because Second Impact was when they were born.

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Sat Aug 08, 2009 2:44 pm so at one moment they where jsut a part of adam, and when he went earth shattering kaboom the "pieces" landed in certain places and voila they turned into angels?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Aug 08, 2009 3:33 pm There's an image during the Rei clone scene in the NPC episode that look like eggs. Presumably, these were the Angels, which were probably somewhere in the white moon. Adam was in the white moon, Adam's Guf was opened, the angels were given souls, Adam exploded, the eggs were blown all over the planet and some into space.

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Sat Aug 08, 2009 3:36 pm Interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 08, 2009 6:23 pm Ornette wrote: There's an image during the Rei clone scene in the NPC episode that look like eggs.

[]: Re: Where were the Angels?             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Aug 13, 2009 12:18 am DEMOLITION D+ wrote: Before they all decided to show up, is it ever revealed via classified files or something like that where the Angels have been hiding this whole time? I know a majority of them come out of the ocean, so I think the bottom of the ocean would be a good guess. But then again there were the angels that appeared in orbit, and there's Tabris.

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Thu Aug 13, 2009 1:01 am Chuck Norris made the Angels, and he is the real instigator of third impact, just to end the Chuck Norris jokes once and for all.

[]: Re: Where were the Angels?             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Aug 13, 2009 4:01 am Allemann wrote: Also: Why do people call their prenatal shells eggs? Cocoon or chrysalis would be more adequate.

[]: Re: Where were the Angels?             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Aug 13, 2009 4:34 am Legendary wrote: Allemann wrote: Also: Why do people call their prenatal shells eggs? Cocoon or chrysalis would be more adequate.

[]: Re: Where were the Angels?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Aug 13, 2009 5:55 am Allemann wrote: But if the angels as souls were preexisting in Adam, they didn't form in an egg.

[]: Re: Where were the Angels?             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Aug 13, 2009 6:22 am Reichu wrote: Allemann wrote: But if the angels as souls were preexisting in Adam, they didn't form in an egg.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Aug 13, 2009 1:17 pm In the Evangelion universe, all human souls (and other animal/plant/fungi/protozoa/eubacteria/archaebacteria) were pre-existing in Lilith, yet there in that universe, the prenatal shell a bird/reptile/what-have-you hatches out of is called an egg, not a cocoon.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Aug 13, 2009 1:36 pm Legendary wrote: In the Evangelion universe, all human souls (and other animal/plant/fungi/protozoa/eubacteria/archaebacteria) were pre-existing in Lilith, yet there in that universe, the prenatal shell a bird/reptile/what-have-you hatches out of is called an egg, not a cocoon.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Aug 13, 2009 1:51 pm The fact that Pen^2 participates in Instrumentality. Some translations of Episode 24 have Kaworu state that every living thing has an AT Field. The Komm Susser Todd sequence is IMMENSELY barren when the crosses are appearing. If the plants were surviving, they'd... be visible. Another strike against cocoon/chrysalis is that they're both clearly middle stages. Something with a body makes it, stays in it, emerges. There's no evidence that the Angels had free-roaming larval bodies before they emerged from the sea/volcano/depths of outer space/protein walls/clouds/god-knows-what-else. As such, people equate the shells with eggs, because that works better. If Anno comes out and says the Angels had free-roaming forms before the shells, we'll call them cocoons. Right now, it's kind of counterintuitive.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Aug 13, 2009 2:18 pm Legendary wrote: The fact that Pen^2 participates in Instrumentality. Some translations of Episode 24 have Kaworu state that every living thing has an AT Field. The Komm Susser Todd sequence is IMMENSELY barren when the crosses are appearing. If the plants were surviving, they'd... be visible.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Aug 13, 2009 2:32 pm Just because their souls existed doesn't mean they have personal identities. They were unborn. Call the womb a chrysalis for the human fetus. Go on.
summary:
[]: Where were the Angels?             Quote Post by DEMOLITION D+ » Sat Aug 08, 2009 8:03 am Before they all decided to show up, is it ever revealed via classified files or something like that where the Angels have been hiding this whole time? I know a majority of them come out of the ocean, so I think the bottom of the ocean would be a good guess. But then again there were the angels that appeared in orbit, and there's Tabris.

[]: &n
